content.problemStatement:
You are given 333 numbers A,BA, BA,B and CCC.

You want to make all these 333 numbers equal. To do this, you can perform any finite number of operations:


In the ithi^{th}ith operation, you must add 2(i−1)2^{(i-1)}2(i−1) to any **one** of these 333 numbers.
Find whether you can make these 333 numbers equal in finite number of moves.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains of a single line of input, three integers A,B,A, B,A,B, and CCC.


Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` if you can make these 333 numbers equal, `NO` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YES`, `yEs`, `yes`, and `yeS` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers A, B and C.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if it is possible to make the three numbers equal using the described operations, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we compute mx and the three differences. The sum of the differences equals the bitwise OR of the differences exactly when the required increments have no overlapping bits, which holds for the first, second and fourth cases and fails for the third.

solution.approach: For a target value X we would need to add (X‑A), (X‑B) and (X‑C) to the three numbers. Each operation adds a distinct power of two to exactly one number, therefore the three required increments must be representable as sums of distinct powers of two with no power used twice. This is possible iff the binary representations of the three increments have no overlapping 1‑bits. The smallest feasible target is the current maximum mx = max(A,B,C). If for this mx the three increments d1 = mx‑A, d2 = mx‑B, d3 = mx‑C satisfy d1 + d2 + d3 == (d1 | d2 | d3) (i.e. no carries when adding them), then the bits are disjoint and the answer is YES; otherwise it is impossible. The check is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.
